folio.label: 170v
folio.l: me vyleynye , and alle othere , to do me a nother vyleynye

for it is wryten , If æè take no vengeau~ce , of an old vileynye ,
æè sompnest æine aduäsaries , to do the a newe vileynye ,

And
also for my suffrau~ce , men wolden do me , so muche vileynye ,
æ I mygút neither bere it , ne sustene ,

And so shulde I ben put
& holden ouä lowe

¢ For so~me men seyn , In muchel suffrynge
shuln manye thynges falle vn to the , whicú æè shalt nat
mowe suffre

¢ Certes q¶ Prudence , I gunte ©ow wel , æ ouä
muchel suffrau~ce , is nat good ,

but ©et ne folwetú it nat æä
of , æ euäy ¹sone , to whom men don vileynye , take of it venge-
aunce ,

for æ a¹tenetú & longetú al oonly to the Iuges , For
æei shuln venge the vileynyes , & iniuries ,

and æäfore æo two
auctoritees , æ ©e han seid a boue , ben oonly vnderstonden in the
Iuges

¢ For whan they suffren , ouä muchel the wronges and
vileynyes to be doon , w outen punysshy~ge ,

they sompne
nat a man al oonly , for to do newe wronges , but æei comaun-
den it

¢ Also as a wys man seitú , æ the Iuge , æ correcteth nat
the sy~nere , comaundetú & biddetú hi~ , do synne

¢ And æe
Iuges & souäeyns mygúten in here hand , so muche suffre
of the shrewes & mysdoeris ,

æ they shulden by swicú suffrau~ce
by ¸ces of tyme , wexen of swicú power & mygút , æ they
shulde putte out the Iuges , & the souäeyns , from here pla-
ces ,
& atte laste , maken he~ lese here lordshippes

¢ But now ,
lat vs now putte , æ ©e haue leue to venge ©ow ,

I seye , ©e
be nat of mygút & power , as now , to venge ©ow ,

For if ©e
wol maken co~parisou~ , vn to the mygút of ©ourö aduäsaries
©e shuln fynde in many thynges , æ I haue shewed ©ow er
this , æ here condicio~n , is bettre than ©oures ,

And æäfore seye
I , æ it is good as now , æ ©e suffre , & be pacient

¢ Foræämore
©e knowen wel , æ after the comune sawe , it is a woodnes-
se , a man to stryue w a strengere , or a more mygúty man
than he is hi~ self ,

And for to stryue w a man , of euene
strengthe , æat is to seyn ; w as stronge a man as he is , it
is ¹il ,
& for to stryue w a weykere man , it is folye ,

And æäfore
shulde a man , fle stryuynge , as muchel as he mygúte

¢ For
Salamou~ seitú ; It is a gret worship~ to a man , to kepe hi~
from noyse & stryf

¢ And if it so happe , æ a man of gretterö
mygút & strengthe , than æè art , do the greuau~ce ,

Studie and
bisie the rather to stille the same greuau~ce , than for to ven-
ge the

¢ For Senek seitú ; æ he puttetú hi~ in a gret ¹il ,
æ stryuetú w a grettere man , æan he is hi~ self

¢ And Ca-
tou~ seitú ; If a man of heigúer estat or degree , or more my©ty
